metadata.title: OpenCompliance Medium Corridor Catalog
metadata.remarks: Synthetic public-safe control catalog for the ExampleCo medium corridor. This is OpenCompliance seed content, not framework text.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Administrative identities require MFA and conditional access in the scoped production environment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Infrastructure access in the scoped production environment requires unique named identities with no shared administrative accounts.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Scoped password-quality controls enforce strong minimum length, no maximum-length restriction, and common-password blocking.

controls.title: Typed periodic access review export present

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A typed export of periodic access review evidence is present for the current review window.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Access review closure and high-risk exception resolution are attested for the current review window.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Scoped customer and environment boundaries are enforced with no undeclared cross-environment paths in the production slice.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A managed web application firewall is attached to the scoped public ingress path, runs in blocking mode, and has a managed rule set active.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Audit logging is enabled with a declared retention window for the scoped production runtime.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Scoped runtime telemetry is forwarded to the declared central monitoring sink with detection rules enabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Encryption at rest is enabled for the scoped customer data stores and no unencrypted store is present.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Scoped runtime service accounts do not use user-managed long-lived keys.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped workload runs only in the declared approved regions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Backup snapshots are scheduled with a declared immutable retention window.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security awareness completion is documented for the current review period.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A restore exercise is documented inside the declared review window.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The processor register is current and DPA coverage is declared for the reviewed subprocessors.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The DSR runbook is approved and has a named owner for the current review window.

controls.title: Privacy by design adequacy review boundary

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Privacy-by-design adequacy has been demonstrated for the full product change process.

controls.title: Lawful basis and purpose register current

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The lawful-basis register is current and records purpose boundaries for the in-scope personal-data processing.

controls.title: Privacy notice and rights contact published

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The external privacy notice is published and links people to the current rights-handling path.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A current privacy impact assessment exists for the in-scope product and names a sign-off owner.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Default branch protections are enforced for the scoped production repository.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: CI workflow policy constrains deployment to reviewed workflows and protected refs.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The production change-review policy is approved and declares an emergency change path for the current review window.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Monitoring review cadence, ownership, and retention operations are attested for the scoped production telemetry program.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The incident response runbook is approved, owned, and current for the declared review window.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Incident escalation contacts and notification paths are current for the declared review window.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: High-risk vendor agreements carry the required security and privacy terms for the current review window.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Vendor tiering, review cadence, and inherited-control allocation are adequate for the in-scope dependency set.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Data retention schedules and exception handling are documented and current for the scoped lifecycle program.

controls.title: Data retention and deletion adequacy boundary

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Retention periods, exception scope, and deletion adequacy are sufficient for the in-scope data lifecycle surface.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Scoped service and supplier agreements carry the declared security commitment terms for the current review window.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Ownership, review cadence, and exception handling are current for scoped security commitment governance.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped security commitments and their governance are adequate for the supplier and transfer risks in scope.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Outsourced development relationships carry current security requirements for the declared review window.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Oversight and exception handling are current for scoped outsourced development relationships.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The outsourced development model is adequate for the scoped third-party delivery risks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A current intake path exists for reported security concerns relevant to the scoped service operations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Tracking, ownership, and resolution follow-up are current for the scoped security-concern process.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped handling model for reported security concerns is adequate to triage, resolve, and learn from the issues that matter.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Scoped remote access traverses the declared remote-access path with device posture controls bounded for the systems in scope.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A current remote-working procedure covers incident reporting for the scoped workforce and review window.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped remote-working model is adequate for the device, workspace, and incident-reporting risks that matter.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A current register of legal, regulatory, contractual, and compliance requirements is attested for the scoped ISMS surface.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Compliance requirement review cadence, ownership, and update handling are current for the scoped inventory.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped compliance inventory is adequate for the legal, regulatory, and contractual obligations that materially affect the ISMS.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A current register of interested parties and stakeholder groups is attested for the scoped ISMS surface.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Stakeholder expectations, obligations, and review ownership are current for the scoped ISMS surface.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped stakeholder-management model is adequate for the interested parties and expectations that materially affect the ISMS.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Current project-security requirements are defined for the project types in the scoped delivery portfolio.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Project-security review and exception tracking are current for the scoped delivery portfolio.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped project-security model is adequate for integrating security into the project types and lifecycle stages that matter.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Current customer support channels are documented and reachable for the scoped service surface.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Support staffing, escalation ownership, and response governance are current for the scoped service surface.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped customer-support model is adequate for the channel, escalation, and service risks that matter.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A current intellectual-property policy or requirement set is attested for the scoped service and workforce surface.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Issue tracking, handling rules, and ownership are current for scoped intellectual-property obligations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped intellectual-property posture is adequate to protect proprietary material and avoid misuse of third-party rights in scope.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A current corrective-action process is defined with named ownership for the scoped ISMS improvement cycle.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Corrective-action follow-up and remediation ownership are current for the scoped improvement cycle.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped continual-improvement model is adequate to identify, prioritize, and close the issues that matter.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A current code-of-conduct acknowledgement set exists for the scoped workforce population.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Refresh triggers and disciplinary pathways are current for the scoped workforce conduct program.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped workforce conduct model is adequate for the behavioural and security risks that matter.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A current disciplinary policy covers the scoped workforce populations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Disciplinary action records are current for the scoped breach-review window.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped disciplinary model is adequate to respond proportionately and consistently when relevant breaches occur.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A current internal-audit program and schedule exist for the scoped control surface.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Findings and follow-up are current for the scoped internal-audit cycle.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped internal-audit model is adequate to provide independent, useful assurance over the controls that matter.

controls.title: Clear desk and screen policy attestation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A current clear-desk and clear-screen policy exists for the scoped work areas.

controls.title: Clear desk and screen coverage attestation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Communication and coverage are current for the scoped clear-desk and clear-screen program.

controls.title: Clear desk and screen adequacy boundary

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped workspace hygiene model is adequate to reduce inadvertent physical disclosure and shoulder-surfing risks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Current participation exists in the scoped external security or special-interest groups.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Ownership and dissemination are current for scoped external security inputs.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The scoped security-community participation model is adequate to turn relevant external insights into useful internal action.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Production change control decomposes into machine policy checks plus signed governance evidence.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Public ingress is HTTPS-only with managed certificates and TLS 1.2 or higher.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The managed network boundary is attached to the declared ingress path for scoped public services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Administrative ingress is restricted to approved source ranges at the managed network boundary.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Plaintext transport is disabled on the scoped public ingress path.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Configuration exceptions are current, owned, and not already expired in the current review window.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Patch exceptions declare compensating controls and no declared exception is already expired in the current review window.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Patch cadence and exception thresholds are adequate for the in-scope production assets.
